title: Use writing-mode for vertical Web-facing Chinese Text

detect.signal: Web-facing Chinese Text is visually rotated with CSS transforms such as `transform: rotate(90deg)` or `rotate: 90deg` instead of using CSS vertical writing properties.
ignore_when: The rotated element is a decorative logo, icon, badge, chart label, or short visual ornament rather than readable Web-facing Chinese Text.
The rotation is part of an intentional animation or art direction and the text also has an accessible, readable alternative.
The text is a short UI tab label whose layout has been reviewed and documented by the design system.
A Project Override documents that visual rotation is required for this component.
The text is not Web-facing Chinese Text.

rationale: CSS transforms rotate the painted box, but they do not make the text participate in vertical writing layout. For readable Web-facing Chinese Text, CSS writing modes let layout, line progression, punctuation, and mixed Latin text be reviewed as text rather than as a rotated graphic.

clreq.title: Requirements for Chinese Text Layout (CLReq)
clreq.section: Mixed text composition in vertical writing mode

web_platform.title: CSS Writing Modes Level 4
web_platform.section: Block Flow Direction: the writing-mode property

agent_guidance.comment_template:
[normal] clreq.vertical-writing.avoid-transform-rotate: Web-facing Chinese Text is rotated visually instead of using vertical writing layout.

Why it matters: CSS transforms rotate the painted box, but vertical writing needs text layout behavior for line progression, punctuation, and mixed Latin or numeric text.
Suggested change: Use `writing-mode` with an appropriate vertical direction and review `text-orientation` for Latin text and numerals; keep visual rotation only for decorative or explicitly reviewed cases.
Source: CSS Writing Modes Level 4, writing-mode and text-orientation; CLReq, Writing modes.
Confidence: medium
agent_guidance.autofix_notes: Do not replace rotation automatically. The correct `writing-mode` direction, `text-orientation`, and layout containment depend on the component, region, and design system.
agent_guidance.decision_notes: Ask for a human decision when the vertical direction, reading order, or decorative-vs-readable role is unclear. If the element is purely decorative and has a readable alternative, suppress or downgrade the suggestion.
